novel_features.name: AI Visibility Audit (flagship)
novel_features.description: One command runs the full BotSee audit: idempotent on existing sites, bootstraps customer types and personas and questions if missing, then runs analysis across every LLM and prints a structured visibility report.
novel_features.rationale: Mimics the proven Python create-site+analyze chain as a single Go command with idempotency on existing sites (normalized-domain match against GET /sites before creation) and local SQLite caching.
novel_features.why_it_matters: This is THE command. Reach for it when an agent or user says 'audit my AI visibility' or 'check how AI sees example.com' — it handles the full lifecycle.

novel_features.name: Recommendations (top-level)
novel_features.description: Generate next-step recommendations from an analysis — promoted to top-level so agents can discover it without descending into the analysis subcommand tree.
novel_features.rationale: Wraps POST /analysis/{uuid}/recommendations and caches the response in the local SQLite store keyed by analysis_uuid.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use after an analysis to get LLM-generated action items without re-spending if you already pulled them once.

novel_features.name: Site config tree
novel_features.description: Print the full customer-types -> personas -> questions tree for a site, with UUIDs and the actual edit commands users can copy-paste to add, update, or remove any node.
novel_features.rationale: Joins customer_types + personas + questions from local SQLite into a nested view that no single BotSee endpoint returns; emits actionable edit hints alongside the tree.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a user asks 'what is set up for this site' or 'show me my BotSee config' — it surfaces every UUID needed for follow-up edits.

novel_features.name: Cross-site sources rollup
novel_features.description: Aggregate cited sources across every synced site, grouped by domain, with citation count, distinct sites citing each domain, and first-seen timestamp.
novel_features.rationale: BotSee's API is strictly per-site; portfolio-level domain authority is only computable in a local store that holds every synced analysis_sources row.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for multi-site users and agencies who need the cross-portfolio answer 'which sources keep getting cited everywhere' — impossible without local aggregation.

narrative.headline: Every BotSee API operation, plus a one-command AI visibility audit that handles the full lifecycle — site, customer types, personas, questions, analysis, results — and a local SQLite cache so agents re-read at zero credit cost.
narrative.value_prop: BotSee is the API-first GEO platform; this CLI is the API-first front door. The flagship `ai-visibility-audit <url>` mirrors the proven Python create-site+analyze flow as a single idempotent Go command. The other 64 commands cover the entire BotSee surface (CRUD on sites/customer-types/personas/questions, the full analysis lifecycle, recommendations, blog content, billing, signup including USDC/x402, webhooks, api-key rotation, usage) plus framework commands (sync/search/sql/doctor) over a local SQLite cache.
narrative.auth_narrative: BotSee uses Bearer tokens prefixed `bts_live_`. Set `BOTSEE_API_KEY` in your environment and the CLI threads it into every authenticated call. Manage keys with `api-keys list / create / rotate / reset / delete` — `reset` consumes a one-time token emailed to the account holder if the key was lost. The CLI never logs key values.

quickstart.comment: Verify auth, reachability, and remaining rate-limit budget
Flagship — bootstrap a new site, run analysis, print results. Idempotent: a second run on the same domain reuses the existing site and just runs a fresh analysis.
Inspect the customer-types / personas / questions tree for the site (with copy-paste edit commands)
Generate next-step recommendations from the analysis (cached locally)
Cross-site cited-source rollup — useful once you've audited multiple domains

troubleshoots.fix: Confirm `BOTSEE_API_KEY` is set and starts with `bts_live_`. Rotate via `botsee-pp-cli api-keys rotate <id>` if compromised.

troubleshoots.symptom: `ai-visibility-audit` says site exists but I expected a fresh setup
troubleshoots.fix: Default behavior is idempotent: reuses the existing site by normalized domain match. Pass `--regenerate` to force LLM re-generation of customer types and personas, or use a different domain.

troubleshoots.symptom: Cost estimate looks too high or too low
troubleshoots.fix: The estimator reads `cost_multiplier` from the live `/pricing` endpoint; if BotSee adjusts it, your estimates adjust automatically. Use `--estimate-only` to preview without spending.
The 600 req/min budget is per key. Add `--max-pages 5` to `sync`, or check the X-RateLimit-Reset header via `rate-limits get`.
x402 challenges return a 402 with payment requirements; the CLI prints the `payTo` address. Pay from a wallet (Pinch, Coinbase CDP), then re-run with `--payment <proof>`.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when an agent or pipeline needs structured BotSee data without burning credits on repeat reads. Best for: running a fresh AI-visibility audit (`ai-visibility-audit <url>` is the headline), inspecting an existing site's setup (`site-config`), generating recommendations from a completed analysis, or aggregating cited sources across multiple sites for a portfolio view. Not the right tool for hosted dashboard UI use — that's `app.botsee.io`.

recipes.title: Audit a new domain end-to-end
recipes.explanation: Creates the site, generates structure, runs analysis, writes a markdown report. Re-running on the same domain just runs a fresh analysis (no duplicate site).

recipes.title: Preview the cost before running
recipes.explanation: Returns predicted credit + USD cost without spending — reads the live `/pricing` endpoint for current multipliers.

recipes.title: Inspect what's configured for a site
recipes.explanation: Returns the nested tree as JSON — agents can walk it to find UUIDs for follow-up `personas update / questions delete` calls.

recipes.title: Find sources getting cited across every brand
recipes.explanation: Cross-site domain authority rollup — impossible via the BotSee API which is per-site only.

recipes.title: Search every LLM response for a competitor mention
recipes.explanation: FTS5 over every raw LLM response synced locally — narrows the deeply-nested response payload to just the fields agents need.

trigger_phrases: what AI is saying about my brand
